title: Cooks, Institution and Cafeteria
description: Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias.

tasks.text: Monitor and record food temperatures to ensure food safety.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Cook foodstuffs according to menus, special dietary or nutritional restrictions, or numbers of portions to be served.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Wash pots, pans, dishes, utensils, or other cooking equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apportion and serve food to facility residents, employees, or patrons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and inspect galley equipment, kitchen appliances, and work areas to ensure cleanliness and functional operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Clean, cut, and cook meat, fish, or poultry.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct activities of one or more workers who assist in preparing and serving meals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take inventory of supplies and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Requisition food supplies, kitchen equipment, and appliances, based on estimates of future needs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bake breads, rolls, or other pastries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor use of government food commodities to ensure that proper procedures are followed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Plan menus that are varied, nutritionally balanced, and appetizing, taking advantage of foods in season and local availability.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor menus and spending to ensure that meals are prepared economically.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Compile and maintain records of food use and expenditures.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Determine meal prices, based on calculations of ingredient prices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
